content:
I am a full-stack/backend-heavy developer who is working on a greenfield personal project in next.js and tailwind. This project is mostly being used for learning a stack I'm less familiar with, but is practical (tldr: leaderboard for video game/arcade leagues).
I'm at the point where I have an MVP of a basic application with barebones html and am ready to start thinking about styling before I get too much bigger. I'm not unfamiliar with CSS or Tailwind, but I am unfamiliar with how to think about structuring CSS from the ground up and what the best practices are. The thing I lack is good design instinct, basically.
For example, Tailwind ships with Preflight, which removes basically all styling to ensure things work the same in all browser. This means something as granular as having to define font sizes for each header type, link styling, etc. So stuff I'm thinking about is -
- do I choose what font I want to use first? What fonts are even available for me to use freely?
- From there, what's best to define first: my baseline paragraph font size and then scale from there?
- What about font-weight, what's "standard"?
- How and where do I define the general width of my application?
- What should be global vs what should be defined per component?
And so on. It's not really the nitty gritty how to do any of the above, so much as how do I think about CSS design from the ground up for my application so I don't make a mess of things.
Any resource suggestions are welcome!

  submitted by   /u/driver194
[link]   [comments]
